category: sold-out product with a stale price still displayed

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded variant/footer data. Price currency is supported by embedded variant data ('22.50 USD'). Availability is supported by 'SOLD OUT' and available=false. Boolean inferences are supported: not a blend (single origin Ethiopia/Halo Beriti), not espresso (recommended brew is filter/flat-bottom brewer), not decaf (no decaf indication), not coferment/infused (standard washed process description only), and specialty coffee is supported by the roaster context and detailed single-origin product presentation.

realism.reasons: This is highly plausible as an html-to-text rendering of a real specialty coffee roaster product page. The structure is very natural: header/navigation, product title, price, size selector, sold-out state, descriptive copy, brew recommendation, related products, variant block, and footer all read like a standard Shopify-style roaster page. The coffee details are coherent and domain-appropriate: Halo Beriti smallholders in Gedeb/Gedeo/Yirgacheffe, washed process, Ethiopian landrace, altitude range, and a 2024/25 harvest all fit real specialty-coffee conventions. The tasting notes and roast-language are believable and restrained rather than exaggerated. The processing description is also realistic for a washed Ethiopian lot. The variant data block looks convincing, with a single 250g variant, SKU formatting, price, inventory=0, available=false, and sold-out UI state all aligning correctly. Minor tiny tells exist only in the polish level: 'default_for_inference' is not something a consumer-facing extraction would usually show, and 'Harvest: 2024/25' plus a © 2026 footer is slightly worth noticing timing-wise, but neither is implausible. Overall, this is very close to indistinguishable from a real roaster product page.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: jasmine, Meyer lemon, white peach, honey

A clean and luminous washed Ethiopia from the Halo Beriti collection site in Gedeb. In the cup we find lifted jasmine aromatics, Meyer lemon brightness, white peach sweetness, and a long honeyed finish. We roast this profile lightly to keep the florals transparent and the acidity crisp.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: jasmine, Meyer lemon, white peach, honey
label.coffee.producer_text: Halo Beriti serves smallholder growers farming garden plots around the highland kebeles of Gedeb. Ripe cherries are delivered daily, depulped, fermented under water, washed clean, and dried on raised beds with careful sorting throughout the drying period.
